originalTitle: Japan core inflation rate in June creeps up from 4-year low as higher oil prices bite
summary: This is the first rise in core inflation since March, and was in line with the1.6% growth expected by economists polled by Reuters.
content:
Japan's core inflation came in at 1.6% in June, as higher oil prices start to spill over into the wider economy.

This is the first rise in core inflation since March, and was in line with the 1.6% growth expected by economists polled by Reuters. Core inflation in Japan strips out prices of fresh food. Headline inflation increased to 1.7% from May's 1.5%, while the so-called "core-core" inflation rate, which strips out prices of fresh food and energy, dipped to 1.7%, the lowest since August 2022. Cushioned by government subsidies, energy prices dipped just 0.1% year-on-year in June, compared with a 2.5% fall in May. Charges for fuel, light and water remained flat, ending 6 straight months of declines.

While Japan has rolled out subsidies to mitigate the impact of the global jump in energy prices for consumers, businesses have seen a sharp rise in costs due to the price spikes, with the producer price index for June hitting 7.1%, highest level since March 2023.

Japan has been struggling with higher energy prices as the Middle East crisis has hit supplies, while historic weakness in the yen has pushed up import costs.

Trade data released Wednesday showed that petroleum imports by the country in terms of value surged by more than 59% year on year, as costs stay elevated. Japan meets over 87% of its energy needs via imports, according to the International Energy Agency.

Weak yen has raised concerns that Japan will need to deal with higher imported inflation. Yen , which has been trading at multi-decade lows, was flat at 163.82 against the dollar on Friday. The country's benchmark Nikkei 225 stock index was 2.14% lower after the data release.

On Wednesday, Reuters, citing sources familiar with the Bank of Japan's thinking, reported that the central bank "remains on alert to upside inflation risks that could lead to faster interest rate hikes than markets ‌project." The report said some within the BOJ see a possibility to raise rates faster if price pressures from a weak yen and rising fuel costs from the Iran conflict push ​up inflation at a faster-than-expected pace.
chineseTitle: 由于油价上涨，日本 6 月份核心通胀率从 4 年低点攀升
chineseSummary: 这是核心通胀自3月份以来首次上升，与路透社调查的经济学家预期的1.6%的增长相符。
chineseContent:
随着油价上涨开始蔓延至更广泛的经济领域，日本 6 月份的核心通胀率为 1.6%。

这是核心通胀自3月份以来首次上升，与路透社调查的经济学家预期的1.6%的增长一致。日本的核心通胀消除了新鲜食品的价格。总体通胀率从 5 月份的 1.5% 升至 1.7%，而剔除新鲜食品和能源价格的所谓“核心核心”通胀率则降至 1.7%，为 2022 年 8 月以来的最低水平。在政府补贴的支撑下，6 月份能源价格同比仅下降 0.1%，而 5 月份则下降了 2.5%。燃料、照明和水费保持不变，结束了连续六个月的下降。

尽管日本推出补贴措施以减轻全球能源价格上涨对消费者的影响，但企业却因价格飙升而成本急剧上升，6月份生产者价格指数触及7.1%，为2023年3月以来的最高水平。

由于中东危机影响了供应，日本一直在努力应对能源价格上涨的问题，而日元的历史性疲软推高了进口成本。

周三公布的贸易数据显示，由于成本居高不下，该国石油进口额同比飙升超过 59%。根据国际能源署的数据，日本 87% 以上的能源需求通过进口满足。

日元疲软引发了人们的担忧，即日本将需要应对更高的输入性通胀。日元兑美元汇率一直处于数十年来的低点，周五兑美元汇率持平于163.82。数据公布后，该国基准日经 225 股票指数下跌 2.14%。

周三，路透社援引熟悉日本央行想法的消息人士的话报道称，该央行“对通胀上行风险保持警惕，这可能导致加息速度快于市场预期”。报告称，日本央行内部的一些人认为，如果日元疲软带来的物价压力和伊朗冲突导致的燃料成本上升以快于预期的速度推高通胀，那么日本央行有可能加快加息。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
